author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
title: John of Constantinople.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: He quotes also John, bishop of Constantinople, as saying “that sin is not a substance, but a wicked act.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: “And because it is not natural, therefore the law was given against it, and because it proceeds from the liberty of our will.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: However, the present question concerns our human nature in its corrupted state; it is a further question also concerning that grace of God whereby our nature is healed by the great Physician, Christ, whose remedy it would not need if it were only whole.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: And yet your author defends it as capable of not sinning, as if it were sound, or as if its freedom of will were self-sufficient.
